title: aeluropus littoralis var. mesopotamicus
intro: Aeluropus littoralis var. mesopotamicus, also known as Mesopotamian saltwort or Mesopotamian sea-blite, is a type of plant that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. It is a halophytic plant, meaning it thrives in environments with high salt concentrations, often found in coastal areas and salt marshes. The medicinal properties of Aeluropus littoralis var. mesopotamicus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its health benefits include reducing stress, promoting digestion, and supporting the immune system.
